profile.label: Sample freelance studio
profile.privacy: Synthetic example. Real business profiles belong under private/profile/ and must not be committed.

operatingFacts.statement: The business provides design and software consulting services to small organizations.
The user works from a dedicated home workspace part of the year, but the allocation method is not confirmed.
The vehicle is occasionally used for client meetings. A mileage log is required before expense treatment.
Business meals should stay in CPA review unless the business purpose, attendees, and current-year limitation are documented.

userApprovedRules.description: Business software subscriptions used only for client work can default to 100 percent business use when an invoice or statement supports the charge.

professionalReviewQuestions.question: Confirm whether the home-office allocation should be used and which method applies.
Confirm whether mileage or actual vehicle expenses should be used after reviewing the mileage log.

agentInstructions: Treat this file as durable local context for future sessions.
Do not add personal identifiers, account numbers, exact private balances, or source-document contents to the public example.
When a rule is user-approved or CPA-approved in real work, record who approved it, when, and what source supports it.
If no rule applies, keep the ledger row reviewable instead of forcing a final category.
